name: Hike from Villar del Buey - circular loop through the Riveras del Campo and Belén
name_original: Salce desde Villar del Buey — circular por las Riveras del Campo y Belén
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Villar Del Buey are set within the diverse landscapes of the Sayago region in Zamora, Spain. This area is characterized by its location between the Duero and Tormes rivers, featuring deep gorges and dramatic scenery within the Arribes del Duero Natural Park. The terrain includes a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, with traditional Sayaguesa houses and the Almendra reservoir adding to the environment. Best mountain bike trails around Villar Del Buey The most popular mountain bike trail is Duero Trail, a 13.1 miles (21.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 56 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Duero river basin, offering varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Arribes and Mills of Sayago Loop, a moderate 25.4 miles (40.9 km) path. This route explores the natural park and traditional mills of the Sayago region. Local mountain bikers also love the Villar del Buey desde Bermillo de Sayago — circular por el Embalse de Almendra, a 34.7 miles (55.8 km) trail leading through the area around the Almendra reservoir, often completed in about 4 hours 37 minutes. Mountain biking around Villar Del Buey is defined by the deep gorges of the Duero and Tormes rivers, the expansive Arribes del Duero Natural Park, and the presence of the Almendra reservoir. The network offers options for moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Villar Del Buey are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 20 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Villar Del Buey's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Villar Del Buey?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails around Villar Del Buey, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes primarily feature moderate and difficult ratings, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain bike trails in this area?
A: The mountain bike trails around Villar Del Buey are predominantly rated as moderate or difficult. Out of the 18 available routes, 14 are considered moderate, and 4 are classified as difficult, providing options for riders seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the Villar Del Buey area?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283193181/villar-del-buey-desde-bermillo-de-sayago-circular-por-el-embalse-de-almendra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villar del Buey desde Bermillo de Sayago — circular por el Embalse de Almendra&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 55.8 km loop that takes you through the scenic area around the Almendra reservoir. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018254255/arribes-and-mills-of-sayago-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arribes and Mills of Sayago Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 40.9 km route exploring the natural park and traditional mills.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Villar Del Buey?
A: The terrain around Villar Del Buey is varied, featuring a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces. You'll encounter diverse landscapes, including deep gorges carved by the Duero and Tormes rivers, sections within the Arribes del Duero Natural Park, and areas around the Almendra reservoir. Traditional 'Sayaguesa' houses also blend into the natural surroundings, adding to the unique character of the routes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in the region?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and cultural landmarks. You can explore the dramatic landscapes of the Arribes del Duero Natural Park, with its deep river gorges. Notable viewpoints include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4791099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Staircase Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de las Barrancas&lt;/a&gt;, offering breathtaking panoramas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3539630" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almendra Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is another prominent feature, and you might also encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Cruz&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Villar Del Buey?
A: The mountain bike routes around Villar Del Buey are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the Arribes del Duero Natural Park, and the sense of exploration offered by trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018871162/duero-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Duero Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017750041/ruta-de-los-arrieros-en-sayago-circular-desde-fermoselle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Arrieros en Sayago — circular desde Fermoselle&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 58.4 km route. Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283193181/villar-del-buey-desde-bermillo-de-sayago-circular-por-el-embalse-de-almendra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villar del Buey desde Bermillo de Sayago — circular por el Embalse de Almendra&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 55.8 km.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Villar Del Buey?
A: Given its location in the Sayago region of Zamora, Spain, the best times for mountain biking are typically spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for comfortable exploration of the diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that offer views of the Duero and Tormes rivers?
A: Absolutely. The region is defined by its location between the Duero and Tormes rivers, which have carved out dramatic gorges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018871162/duero-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Duero Trail&lt;/a&gt; directly follows the Duero river basin, offering excellent views. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018268987/los-canones-del-tormes-y-presa-de-almendra-circular-desde-fermoselle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los cañones del Tormes y presa de Almendra — circular desde Fermoselle&lt;/a&gt; route provides stunning vistas of the Tormes canyons.

Q: Can I find trails suitable for a full day of mountain biking?
A: Yes, several routes are ideal for a full day out. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017750041/ruta-de-los-arrieros-en-sayago-circular-desde-fermoselle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Arrieros en Sayago — circular desde Fermoselle&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that typically takes over 5 hours to complete. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283193181/villar-del-buey-desde-bermillo-de-sayago-circular-por-el-embalse-de-almendra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villar del Buey desde Bermillo de Sayago — circular por el Embalse de Almendra&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent option, usually taking around 4 hours 37 minutes.

Q: Are there any specific trails that highlight the Arribes del Duero Natural Park?
A: Many trails traverse the Arribes del Duero Natural Park, allowing you to experience its stunning canyons and natural wonders. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018254255/arribes-and-mills-of-sayago-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arribes and Mills of Sayago Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that specifically explores this natural park and its traditional mills.
tips: Villar del Buey is a small rural town in the Sayago region. Pass by the Church of Santa Marina and rest in the shade of the trees of the Plaza.
The old road passes through the stone bridge over the Salce Rivera on the banks of the Argusino reservoir.
Along the rapids of the Rivera del Campo, on the outskirts of the town of Carbellino, stands the old water mill. The path runs along part of the river so you can enjoy the natural landscape.

Carbellino, a small town in the Sayago region, northwest of the province of Zamora, is located near the Almendra reservoir. You will be able to rest next to the Church of San Miguel Arcángel before continuing with your adventure.
Stone bridge that crosses the Rivera de Belén on the outskirts of the town of Almeida de Sayago. The bridge dates from Roman times, although it has probably been adopted many times since then.

waypoints.name: Church of Santa Marina of Villar del Buey
Stone Bridge over the Salce Rivera
Old Mill of the Rivera del Campo
Church of San Miguel Arcángel of Carbellino
